lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.title: Kick a Lucky Block Codes
lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.intro: Track code status clearly: active codes, expired entries, and whether the in-game redemption menu is currently available.

steps.title: Check Active Codes First
steps.description: Start with a simple active-code status panel. If there are no working codes, state it clearly so players do not waste attempts.

steps.title: Review Expired or Removed Codes
steps.description: Keep an expired section to prevent repeated retries on dead strings copied from old videos and repost pages.

steps.title: Confirm Whether a Code Box Exists
steps.description: If the game UI has no code-entry field, call that out directly so players understand why every pasted code fails.

steps.title: Filter Out Fake Reward Claims
steps.description: Ignore posts promising instant gems, spins, or money boosts unless they match an actual in-game redemption flow.

steps.title: Watch Official Drop Channels
steps.description: Monitor Roblox game updates and official community posts for any first-time code rollout announcement.

quickTips: No code box means no code can be redeemed yet.
Retest only when the game UI changes or an official post announces codes.
Keep active and expired lists separate to reduce confusion.
Use plain status language so players can decide fast and keep playing.

lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.title: Kick a Lucky Block Beginner Guide
lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.intro: The first-hour loop is simple: kick, land in a zone, outrun the wave, place your brainrot, and reinvest the payout.

cards.name: Kick at Your Plot
cards.description: Each run starts at your plot. Your current kick power controls how far the lucky block can travel.

cards.name: Push Into Better Zones
cards.description: Farther zones open better brainrot pools. Even small weight upgrades can move you into stronger reward bands.

cards.name: Beat the Tsunami Timer
cards.description: After every kick, return speed matters. If the wave catches you, the run does not convert into plot income.

cards.name: Spend for Momentum
cards.description: Use early cash on weights and run speed so every next run is safer, faster, and more profitable.

milestones: Reach your first consistent return route
Fill your early plot slots with stable earners
Unlock your first meaningful weight jump

lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.title: Kick a Lucky Block Brainrots
lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.intro: Brainrots are your economy engine. Better zones and safer returns give better units, and better units fund faster upgrades.

items.name: Starter Brainrots
items.description: Use early drops to fill empty slots quickly. Slot uptime matters more than perfect rarity in the first phase.

items.name: Mid-Tier Brainrots
items.description: Once your route is reliable, replace weak starters with stronger mid-tier units to smooth upgrade pacing.

items.name: High-Rarity Brainrots
items.description: Mythic-and-above drops accelerate your economy and open faster access to expensive weight jumps.

items.name: Mutated Brainrots
items.description: A strong mutation can outperform a plain higher-tier unit. Compare income value before replacing.

items.name: Plot Slot Optimization
items.description: Keep your strongest earners active. Rotate out low-yield units once better zone access is stable.

items.name: Risk-Adjusted Carry Picks
items.description: During difficult wave routes, favor reliable returns over overly risky long-distance attempts.

items.name: Rebirth Prep Portfolio
items.description: Before rebirth, build a balanced set of strong earners so post-reset recovery is faster.

items.name: Endgame Keep List
items.description: Prioritize top-tier or high-multiplier units that still scale when weight and speed costs rise sharply.

lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.title: Kick a Lucky Block Weights
lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.intro: Weights drive kick power growth. The best route is to buy real power spikes instead of over-investing in tiny steps.

solutions.name: Wooden Stick to Bone Barbell
solutions.description: Move off the default starter quickly and secure your first meaningful power increase.

solutions.name: Stone Block to Copper Plate
solutions.description: This transition decides how soon you can consistently reach better zones and higher-value returns.

solutions.name: Iron and Ice Tier Weights
solutions.description: Mid-tier purchases should align with your run speed so longer kicks remain recoverable.

solutions.name: Donut to Endgame Plates
solutions.description: Late weights are expensive, so combine rebirth multipliers and strong plot income before committing.

managementTips: Buy for breakpoint value, not for cosmetic rarity labels.
Never let kick distance outpace your survival route.
If income stalls, improve brainrot quality before forcing another weight tier.
Tie each major weight purchase to a concrete zone target.

lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.title: Kick a Lucky Block Zones
lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.intro: Learn every zone from Common to Celestial and how Kick Power changes the Brainrot you roll. Higher zones give better Brainrots, but they also make the return run more dangerous.

cards.name: 1. Common (Starter)
cards.description: Kick Power: no training or very low power. Brainrot value: lowest tier. Use it only to start passive income, then move into Rare quickly after early weight upgrades.

cards.name: 2. Rare (Starter)
cards.description: Kick Power: low. Brainrot value: low to moderate. This is an early training stop, so use the income for weight upgrades and prepare for Epic.

cards.name: 3. Epic (Early Game)
cards.description: Kick Power: early upgrades such as Bone Barbell range. Brainrot value: first meaningful tier. Add some Run Speed before pushing farther to survive longer returns.

cards.name: 4. Legend (Early-Mid Game)
cards.description: Kick Power: mid power after stronger early weights. Brainrot value: stronger early-mid earnings. Start replacing weak Common and Rare units here.

cards.name: 5. Mythic (Mid Game)
cards.description: Kick Power: high. Brainrot value: major income jump. Prioritize stronger Mythic pulls and expand plot capacity so valuable rolls are not wasted.

cards.name: 6. Godly (Mid-Late Game)
cards.description: Kick Power: high with stronger weights. Brainrot value: very high, often millions per second. Pair every distance push with Speed upgrades for safer returns.

cards.name: 7. Secret (Late Game)
cards.description: Kick Power: very high. Brainrot value: massive rewards. Do not chase distance alone; only push when your speed can survive the tsunami chase.

cards.name: 8. Divine (Late Game)
cards.description: Kick Power: very high after multiple upgrades. Brainrot value: large multipliers with strong income potential. Use Divine runs to prepare endgame mutation cycles.

cards.name: 9. Hacked (Endgame)
cards.description: Kick Power: endgame. Brainrot value: top-three tier with billion-per-second potential. Time runs around luck windows to maximize mutation value.

cards.name: 10. OG (Endgame+)
cards.description: Kick Power: very high endgame. Brainrot value: billion-plus potential. Farm OG only when Run Speed can support the full return distance.

cards.name: 11. Celestial (Final Endgame)
cards.description: Kick Power: highest tier. Brainrot value: maximum listed value. Best used with stacked rebirth multipliers, strong speed, and active luck boosts.

highlights: Treat zones as a progression ladder from Common to Celestial.
Increase Kick Power only when Run Speed can still secure the return.
Use higher-zone farming to support mutation and rebirth planning.
Replace low-tier plot units aggressively as zone quality improves.

lucidBlocksWorldRegions.title: Kick a Lucky Block Mutations
lucidBlocksWorldRegions.intro: Mutations turn a normal Brainrot into a higher-earning version. Each mutation boosts both cash-per-second income and sale value, making high-zone mutated Brainrots the strongest progression targets.

regions.name: Gold (Golden)
regions.description: Increases earning rate and sale value by 1.5x. Useful early, but replace it when stronger zones and mutations become available.

regions.name: Diamond
regions.description: Doubles earning rate and sale value. Strong on Rare, Epic, and Legend while building toward deeper zones.

regions.name: Plasma
regions.description: Quadruples earning rate and sale value. A strong mid-game keeper when it lands on Mythic or better units.

regions.name: Molten
regions.description: Raises earning rate and sale value by 6x. Prioritize Molten on Godly, Secret, and Divine units for better scaling.

regions.name: Radioactive
regions.description: Raises earning rate and sale value by 8x. This is a major late-game farming spike on already valuable Brainrots.

regions.name: Shadow
regions.description: Raises earning rate and sale value by 12x. Save this tier for high-value zones because the multiplier is too strong for starter units.

regions.name: Electrified
regions.description: Raises earning rate and sale value by 16x. Extremely strong with rebirth cash multipliers in endgame loops.

regions.name: Rainbow
regions.description: Raises earning rate and sale value by 30x. The top listed mutation target for Perfect Kick runs and global luck windows.

lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.title: Kick a Lucky Block Rebirth
lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.intro: Rebirth trades your current Kick Power for permanent cash multipliers. It is the main long-term progression system after you start reaching better zones consistently.

creatures.name: What does Rebirth do?
creatures.description: Rebirth resets your current Kick Power progression and grants a permanent cash multiplier. Later rebirths stack so future Brainrots earn faster.

creatures.name: What is the first Rebirth requirement?
creatures.description: The first rebirth unlocks at 1,000 Kick Power and grants a permanent 2x cash multiplier when confirmed.

creatures.name: What resets when you Rebirth?
creatures.description: Kick Power progression resets, so you train again for distance. Run Speed remains a critical long-term survival upgrade for return routes.

creatures.name: What do you keep after Rebirth?
creatures.description: You keep the cash multiplier, which applies to future earnings and helps you rebuy stronger weights faster after each cycle.

creatures.name: Should you Rebirth immediately at 1,000 Kick Power?
creatures.description: Early rebirth can accelerate progression, but often it is stronger to push one or two zones deeper first so the multiplier amplifies a better income base.

creatures.name: How does the 99 Robux Rebirth Skip work?
creatures.description: Rebirth Skip lets you reset without meeting the current Kick Power requirement. It saves time, while normal progression remains the core upgrade loop.

creatures.name: What is the best Rebirth strategy?
creatures.description: Before rebirthing, fill your plot with better Brainrots, improve Run Speed, and secure stronger weights so every reset returns to high zones faster.

creatures.name: Common Rebirth mistakes
creatures.description: Avoid resetting with a weak plot, ignoring Run Speed, or over-hoarding cash instead of upgrading income and weight progression.

lucidBlocksMobilityGear.title: Kick a Lucky Block Tsunami Survival
lucidBlocksMobilityGear.intro: After every kick, the tsunami chase decides whether your Brainrot becomes real profit or disappears. The farther your Lucky Block lands, the more important Run Speed becomes.

items.name: Kick the Lucky Block
items.description: Start each run from your plot or safe area. Landing distance determines zone quality and the Brainrot tier you can roll.

items.name: Watch the landing distance
items.description: Use the reached zone to estimate return risk. Common through Legend is easier, while Mythic and above create longer and riskier chases.

items.name: Become the Brainrot and turn back fast
items.description: Once the Brainrot appears, return toward safety immediately. Delay can waste a strong roll before it is banked.

items.name: Avoid the tsunami wave
items.description: Move directly back with minimal detours. If the wave catches you before return, the rolled Brainrot is lost.

items.name: Place the Brainrot on your plot
items.description: After reaching safety, place the unit immediately so it starts generating passive cash for progression.

items.name: Upgrade Run Speed before pushing farther
items.description: Spend income on Speed upgrades near the Weight Shop as distance grows. Higher Kick Power without matching Speed is unstable.

items.name: Balance Kick Power and Speed
items.description: Do not chase Secret, Divine, Hacked, OG, or Celestial through raw power alone. Safe conversions beat repeated high-zone losses.

items.name: Use Rebirth and mutations to reduce wasted runs
items.description: Build permanent multipliers through Rebirth and prioritize mutated high-tier rolls you can safely return with.

unlockMilestones: Pair every major distance push with a Run Speed upgrade.
Turn back immediately after each roll to reduce wave losses.
Farm high zones only when return consistency remains stable.
Use rebirth multipliers to accelerate safe Speed and weight scaling.

lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.title: Kick a Lucky Block Run Speed
lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.intro: Run Speed is the survival side of progression. Every longer kick creates a longer return path, so stronger weights should be paired with speed upgrades instead of treated as a late-game luxury.

sections.name: Buy One Early Speed Upgrade
sections.description: After your first few placements, add Run Speed before chasing only weights so your first valuable Rare or Epic roll is less likely to be lost.

sections.name: Match Speed With Weight Progression
sections.description: Weights unlock farther zones, but farther zones also lengthen the run back. Add one Run Speed upgrade after every few meaningful weight upgrades.

sections.name: Protect Mutated Brainrots
sections.description: Mutation rolls only pay off after you return and place them. Better Run Speed converts lucky mutation moments into real passive income.

sections.name: Use Routes, Not Just Raw Speed
sections.description: Run Speed is your stat advantage, but route discipline matters too. Learn the shortest clean return line before grinding deeper zones.

sections.name: Rebuild Speed After Rebirth Momentum
sections.description: Rebirth improves long-term scaling, but claim survival still decides your next climb. Re-balance weights and speed quickly once income restarts.

growthMilestones: Place two or three early Brainrots, then buy a cheap Run Speed level.
Do not let Kick Power advance several zones ahead of movement speed.
During luck windows, farm only zones you can safely return from.
Practice one repeatable shortest route from each new zone.
After rebirth, restore the weight-speed balance as soon as cash flow stabilizes.

lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.title: Kick a Lucky Block Perfect Kick
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.intro: Perfect Kick is the timing skill that connects Kick Power, zone reach, and mutation hunting. Reliable meter control improves every farming session without needing premium boosts.

priorities.name: Start From Your Plot
priorities.description: Stand at your plot, trigger the block, and focus on the timing bar before launch for cleaner and more repeatable setup.

priorities.name: Fill The Green Zone
priorities.description: Land the meter in the green zone instead of releasing early. A full green hit gives a true Perfect Kick with maximum launch power.

priorities.name: Use The Extra Reach
priorities.description: Use the stronger launch to target the farthest zone your current setup can safely convert into completed returns.

priorities.name: Convert The Luck Boost
priorities.description: The kick-specific luck bonus matters only after a successful claim, so sprint the cleanest route back and place immediately.

priorities.name: Stack With Luck Windows
priorities.description: During Global Luck windows, prioritize Perfect Kicks continuously so event luck, kick timing, and mutation attempts compound together.

lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.title: Kick a Lucky Block Luck Events
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.intro: Luck events are strongest when you are prepared before the timer starts. Keep income running, lock in your route, and convert each boosted window into completed claims.

achievements.title: Global Luck Event
achievements.description: When server-wide luck starts, switch from slow setup tasks to rapid repeated kicks from the strongest zone you can safely return from.

achievements.title: 2x Luck Window
achievements.description: Use 2x periods for stable loops: Perfect Kick, return, place, repeat. This is especially efficient while building early and mid-game consistency.

achievements.title: 4x Luck Window
achievements.description: A 4x window is strong enough to justify full focus on kick volume. Minimize shop downtime unless one quick upgrade keeps your loop safe.

achievements.title: 8x Luck Window
achievements.description: Use your best setup only: strongest reachable zone, clean route, enough Run Speed, and disciplined Perfect Kick timing.

achievements.title: Admin Abuse Event Window
achievements.description: Treat special event spikes as premium mutation-hunting bursts and convert as many high-value rolls as possible before the window ends.

achievements.title: Global Luck + Perfect Kick
achievements.description: Use server luck and clean meter timing together so each kick has both stronger launch quality and better event conditions.

achievements.title: Add Kick Power At The Right Pace
achievements.description: Kick Power expands zone reach, but only push higher when Run Speed and route control can still secure returns.

achievements.title: Layer Mutation Luck For Event Bursts
achievements.description: If you are actively mutation farming, combine mutation-focused boosts with event timing rather than using them in low-value idle windows.

lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.title: Kick a Lucky Block Game Passes
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.intro: Game passes can accelerate progression, but value depends on your current bottleneck. Prioritize passes that solve today’s constraint instead of buying everything at once.

faqs.question: When is x2 Cash the right purchase?
faqs.answer: It becomes strong after your plot already has reliable earners and you can recycle extra cash directly into weights, speed, and rebirth recovery.

faqs.question: Why buy x2 Brainrot Luck early to mid game?
faqs.answer: Better base Brainrot outcomes improve every later multiplier and make each successful return more valuable while your economy is still forming.

faqs.question: Who should prioritize x2 Mutation Luck?
faqs.answer: Players who already reach valuable zones and actively farm event windows get the most from this pass because mutation attempts scale with strong routes.

faqs.question: Is VIP a first purchase for progression?
faqs.answer: Usually no. It is better treated as an optional premium add-on after core progression passes like cash, Brainrot luck, mutation luck, or kick power.

faqs.question: When does x2 Kick Power offer the highest value?
faqs.answer: It is most useful when income is stable but distance is blocking access to higher-value zones such as Mythic, Godly, Secret, and beyond.

faqs.question: What is a practical pass-buying order?
faqs.answer: Start with the pass that fixes your current bottleneck, then reassess after a few sessions. Free routes remain viable with good timing, speed balance, and rebirth planning.

lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.title: Kick a Lucky Block AFK Income
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.intro: The strongest AFK route is to lock in high-value Brainrots first, then convert idle cash into meaningful weight and speed milestones.

faqs.question: How should I prepare before AFK training starts?
faqs.answer: Place your best Brainrots on the plot before stepping away. Your passive income only scales if strong earners are already active.

faqs.question: Which weight milestones matter most for AFK growth?
faqs.answer: A practical route is Wooden Stick, Bone Barbell at $7,500, Copper Plate at $500,000, Iron Plate at $7.2M, and Ice Barbell at $350M.

faqs.question: When should I collect offline cash?
faqs.answer: Collect regularly instead of letting the cap sit full. Frequent collection turns stored income into faster progression and safer runs.

faqs.question: What should I buy first after collecting AFK income?
faqs.answer: Spend on the next upgrade that changes outcomes, usually weight for farther zones or run speed to protect valuable returns.

faqs.question: Which Brainrots should get upgrades first?
faqs.answer: Prioritize top-tier or mutated Brainrots. Limited plot slots make wide spending on weak Common and Rare units inefficient.

faqs.question: What is the repeatable AFK money cycle?
faqs.answer: Kick, return safely, place better Brainrots, collect offline income, reinvest, and repeat. That loop compounds both passive cash and kick power.

lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.title: Kick a Lucky Block No Robux Progression
lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.intro: No-Robux progression works when each phase has one clear goal: build income, hit weight breakpoints, protect returns, then rebirth on timing.

settings.name: Step 1: Start With Fast Kick Runs
settings.description: Kick, return before the tsunami, and place each Brainrot quickly. In the opening minutes, consistency beats perfect rarity.

settings.name: Step 2: Buy the First Useful Weight
settings.description: Use free Wooden Stick first, then save for Bone Barbell at $7,500 to improve early zone reach without paid boosts.

settings.name: Step 3: Save for Bigger Jumps
settings.description: After Bone Barbell, prioritize larger jumps like Copper Plate at $500,000, then Iron Plate and Ice Barbell as long-term targets.

settings.name: Step 4: Add Run Speed Before Overextending
settings.description: When zones get farther, run speed protects rare returns and prevents losing valuable Brainrots during wave pressure.

settings.name: Step 5: Upgrade Best Brainrots Only
settings.description: Replace low earners as stronger units arrive, and focus upgrade cash on high-rarity or mutated Brainrots.

settings.name: Step 6: Rebirth at the Right Time
settings.description: First rebirth unlocks at 1,000 Kick Power with a 2x cash multiplier. Rebirth when your base can rebuild quickly, not just when the threshold appears.

lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.title: Kick a Lucky Block Updates
lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.intro: Track dated Roblox and event milestones to decide what to farm first whenever the live progression meta shifts.

entries.title: April 7, 2026: Public Roblox Launch

lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.intro: Use official IDs and live platform metrics to confirm you are tracking the correct experience and active channels.

faq.title: Kick a Lucky Block
faq.subtitle: Quick answers for code status, progression choices, and update tracking.

questions.question: Are there active codes right now?
questions.answer: The code status can change over time, so the fastest check is the homepage code module plus official game announcements.

questions.question: What should I upgrade first: weight or run speed?
questions.answer: If your returns are failing, prioritize speed. If your routes are stable, prioritize weight to unlock better zones.

questions.question: When should I rebirth?
questions.answer: Rebirth when your current route is stable and your reset multiplier will clearly accelerate the next cycle.

questions.question: How do I farm better brainrots consistently?
questions.answer: Push distance gradually, keep return safety high, and replace low-yield units as soon as stronger options stabilize.

questions.question: Do I need game passes to progress well?
questions.answer: No. A disciplined free-to-play path with clean upgrade timing can progress steadily through all major systems.

questions.question: Where should I follow updates?
questions.answer: Use the official Roblox page, developer group, and Discord community for the most direct update flow.

cta.title: Ready for Your Next Upgrade Cycle?
cta.description: Use the module navigation to jump into the exact system you want to optimize next.

codes.title: Kick a Lucky Block Codes
codes.description: Track working codes, expired codes, and redemption menu status in one place.
codes.metaTitle: Kick a Lucky Block Codes - Working and Expired Status
codes.metaDescription: Check the latest Kick a Lucky Block codes status, redemption steps, and where new rewards are announced.
codes.whatIsDescription: Codes are limited promotional rewards shared by the developers. This section tracks whether redemption is currently available, which entries are active, and which ones have expired.

whySteps.step1Description: Use active rewards to speed up early power growth and plot income without extra grind.
whySteps.step2Description: See active and expired status clearly so you do not waste time testing dead entries.
whySteps.step3Description: Track where new rewards are announced so you can redeem them before they expire.

guide.title: Kick a Lucky Block Guide
guide.description: Beginner routes, progression priorities, and daily play loops for faster growth.
guide.metaTitle: Kick a Lucky Block Guide - Beginner to Advanced Progression
guide.metaDescription: Learn efficient Kick a Lucky Block progression, from first kicks to optimized rebirth and income loops.
guide.whatIsDescription: The guide section explains practical progression order: what to do in your first session, when to train power, how to balance risk and income, and how to avoid common stalls.

whySteps.step1Description: Follow a proven opening sequence so every minute improves power, distance, and economy.
whySteps.step2Description: Skip inefficient upgrades by using tested priorities for training, placement, and rebirth timing.
whySteps.step3Description: Transition from basic farming to higher-value routes with fewer resets and better planning.

mutations.title: Kick a Lucky Block Mutations
mutations.description: Mutation effects, value impact, and practical roll strategy by progression stage.
mutations.metaTitle: Kick a Lucky Block Mutations - Effects and Value Comparison
mutations.metaDescription: Compare mutation effects and value impact in Kick a Lucky Block to decide what to keep, reroll, or replace.
mutations.whatIsDescription: Mutations are special modifiers that change Brainrot output and rarity value. Their impact can be large, so understanding priorities is key to stable long-term growth.

whySteps.step1Description: Identify which mutation outcomes deserve long-term slots instead of immediate replacement.
whySteps.step2Description: Use value tiers to decide when rerolling is worth the resources and when to hold.
whySteps.step3Description: Use different mutation targets for early acceleration, midgame consistency, and late-game scaling.

brainrots.title: Kick a Lucky Block Brainrots
brainrots.description: Brainrot lists, rarity/value comparisons, and slot management strategy.
brainrots.metaTitle: Kick a Lucky Block Brainrots - List, Value, and Best Picks
brainrots.metaDescription: Browse Kick a Lucky Block brainrots by rarity and value to choose better placements and replacement targets.
brainrots.whatIsDescription: Brainrots are the core collectible units that generate income on your plot. Their rarity, mutation state, and placement choices directly affect your earning speed.

whySteps.step1Description: Prioritize units that produce stronger returns for each slot in your current progression stage.
whySteps.step2Description: Use value comparisons to remove weak units earlier and free space for better options.
whySteps.step3Description: Create a balanced lineup that supports both daily income and long-term mutation upgrades.

power.title: Kick a Lucky Block Power
power.description: Weights, kick power breakpoints, speed gains, and rebirth pacing.
power.metaTitle: Kick a Lucky Block Power - Weights, Kick Scaling, and Rebirth Timing
power.metaDescription: Plan Kick a Lucky Block power growth with weight upgrades, distance breakpoints, and practical rebirth timing.
power.whatIsDescription: Power progression covers weight upgrades, kick distance scaling, and movement efficiency. Good breakpoint planning lets you reach better zones and rewards sooner.

whySteps.step1Description: Use clear upgrade checkpoints to unlock farther zones without wasted spending.
whySteps.step2Description: Avoid over-investing in one stat by matching training choices to your current farm route.
whySteps.step3Description: Use threshold-based rebirth timing to protect momentum and avoid inefficient resets.

shop.title: Kick a Lucky Block Shop
shop.description: Shop priorities, gifting/trading unlocks, and smart spending order.
shop.metaTitle: Kick a Lucky Block Shop - Best Buy Order and Economy Priorities
shop.metaDescription: Use a practical Kick a Lucky Block shop priority list for upgrades, gifting features, and economy planning.
shop.whatIsDescription: The shop section focuses on purchase order, upgrade value, and utility unlocks such as gifting and trading-related functions. It helps reduce wasteful spending.

whySteps.step1Description: Prioritize upgrades that improve income loops immediately instead of low-impact vanity purchases.
whySteps.step2Description: Track when to unlock gifting and other functions so they support progression rather than delay it.
whySteps.step3Description: Avoid dead-end spending paths that slow your power growth and reset preparation.

leaderboard.title: Kick a Lucky Block Leaderboard
leaderboard.description: Leaderboard patterns, top-player setups, and climb planning.
leaderboard.metaTitle: Kick a Lucky Block Leaderboard - Climb Strategy and Top Meta Patterns
leaderboard.metaDescription: Study Kick a Lucky Block leaderboard trends to improve setup decisions and climb rankings more efficiently.
leaderboard.whatIsDescription: This section tracks leaderboard-focused play: ranking signals, top-player progression patterns, and setup adjustments that help you climb consistently.

whySteps.step1Description: See what top performers prioritize so you can compare your setup against proven standards.
whySteps.step2Description: Use ranking patterns to adjust your mutation, power, and plot decisions with clear goals.
whySteps.step3Description: Turn leaderboard data into repeatable routines instead of random experimentation.
